Baker Beach offers one of the most iconic engagement settings in San Francisco, with sweeping views of the Golden Gate Bridge and unforgettable sunsets over the Pacific.

The combination of open shoreline, soft natural light, and dramatic scenery creates a timeless, elevated backdrop for any special moment.

Sandy Beach at The Presidio of San Francisco offering beautiful views of the Golden Gate Bridge, the Marin Headlands and the Pacific Ocean. It was not busy on the Saturday afternoon in early June that we visited. However, although it was sunny, it was also cool and windy. I suspect it would get much busier on warmer days. We hiked here along the excellent Batteries to Bluffs Trail that branched off the California Coastal Trail that began at the Golden Gate Welcome Center. However, there is a moderate sized parking lot if you wish to drive here. Another option is to catch the #29 bus that stops near the beach. A more secluded beach is Marshall Beach which is just north of here although it requires some effort to reach.

Baker Beach offers one of the most incredible views of the Golden Gate Bridge anywhere in the city.

It’s a perfect spot for photos, sunset watching, or simply enjoying the ocean.
